extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won the sprint after EVENEPOEL's Soudal led into the finale but abandoned the sprint in the final kilometer.
tactical_patterns: Soudal committed Roglic as lead-out pilot for Evenepoel despite GC jersey, revealing over-confidence in their numerical advantage.
Ineos worked entire stage for Hayter but disappeared in final 4km, suggesting positioning failures over power deficit.
Strong launched from barriers with 200m remaining but center-road positioning advantage still delivered Groves the win.
Breakaway composition included two-day repeat Jensen, indicating Jayco using specific rider to burn opposition without GC cost.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Considered sprinting but withdrew late, showing tactical restraint unusual for a GC leader tempted by stage wins.
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: Ineos committed full team resources for four kilometers but Hayter never appeared in final 500m sprint positions.
rider_signals.STRONG Corbin: positive: Launched earliest from worst position (barriers) yet held podium, suggesting explosive power compensates for positioning errors.
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: Executed perfect wheel selection on Coquard despite Alpecin having no train, showing independent sprint intelligence.
